comment_text.sn20.3:1.2: AN 8.51:27.1, Kd 20:1.6.7. | Few men and many women is said to be a sign of the end of the world (Harivaṁśa 116.23, Brahma Purāṇa 231.24, Liṅga Purāṇa 1.40.19, Narasiṁha Purāṇa 54.22 ). | The kumbha (“jar”) was a measure of grain by which the severity of theft was measured (Manu 8.320). Perhaps the implication is that these are house-thieves plundering domestic goods, rather than highway robbers.

root_text.sn20.3:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, yāni kānici kulāni bahutthikāni appapurisāni tāni suppadhaṁsiyāni honti corehi kumbhatthenakehi;
root_text.sn20.3:1.3: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci bhikkhuno mettācetovimutti abhāvitā abahulīkatā so suppadhaṁsiyo hoti amanussehi.
root_text.sn20.3:1.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, yāni kānici kulāni appitthikāni bahupurisāni tāni duppadhaṁsiyāni honti corehi kumbhatthenakehi;
root_text.sn20.3:1.5: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci bhikkhuno mettācetovimutti bhāvitā bahulīkatā so duppadhaṁsiyo hoti amanussehi.
root_text.sn20.3:1.7: ‘mettā no cetovimutti bhāvitā bhavissati bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā’ti.

translation_text.sn20.3:1.2: “Mendicants, those families with many women and few men are easy prey for bandits and  grain-jar thieves.
translation_text.sn20.3:1.3: In the same way any mendicant who has not developed and cultivated the heart’s release by love is easy prey for non-humans.
translation_text.sn20.3:1.4: Those families with few women and many men are hard prey for bandits and grain-jar thieves.
translation_text.sn20.3:1.5: In the same way a mendicant who has developed and cultivated the heart’s release by love is hard prey for non-humans.
translation_text.sn20.3:1.6: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn20.3:1.7: ‘We will develop the heart’s release by love. We’ll cultivate it, make it our vehicle and our basis, keep it up, consolidate it, and properly implement it.’
